mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate on future equity cash flows and supports risk appetite; with FEDFUNDS near the zero lower bound, the mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS below 0.5% is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations, or if the mean return is not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A very high unemployment rate signals persistent slack, weak household income growth, and elevated recession risk; when UNRATE crosses above 9%, the mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales during near-zero policy rates indicate recovering household demand while monetary conditions remain supportive; when RSAFS growth is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the mechanism predicts positive 20-day equity returns.
regime_hint: early-cycle demand recovery with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger has lower average 20-day returns than either RSAFS-only recovery signals or the unconditional sample, or if hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Improving industrial production signals that firms are rebuilding output and earnings capacity; a positive 3-month change in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are not associated with higher subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative changes across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Depressed housing starts indicate weak residential investment, impaired credit creation, and poor construction employment; when HOUST crosses below 700 thousand units, the mechanism predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 700 is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if drawdowns are not larger than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Falling consumer prices while policy rates are already near zero signal deflation risk and limited conventional policy space; when CPIAUCSL momentum is negative and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the mechanism predicts negative 5-day returns.
regime_hint: deflation scare at the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deflation-and-zero-rate trigger does not reduce average 5-day S&P 500 returns relative to zero-rate periods without CPIAUCSL declines.

mechanism_narrative: A turn from payroll contraction to payroll expansion signals that firms are shifting from cost cutting to hiring, raising expected income and earnings; when PAYEMS 3-month change turns positive, the mechanism predicts positive 60-day returns.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS positive-turn triggers fail to produce higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-trigger periods in at least two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP remains weak but retail sales improve, equity investors discount a demand inflection before quarterly output data fully recover; the joint condition predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth < 0 and RSAFS 3-month growth > 0
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with lagging quarterly output
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-GDP and improving-RSAFS condition has a 20-day return hit rate below 50% or does not outperform weak-GDP observations without retail-sales improvement.
